libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment property appears necessary. Tests should avoid removed 1.x APIs such as RenderingContext, SkinResourceLoader, enableLogging, and setUsingDefaultTemplate. Use the 2.0.0 APIs such as DocumentRenderingContext and SiteRenderingContext, obtain an injected renderer through the Plexus/JUnit test support, and create any skin/template input as temporary test files or a temporary skin jar.

deterministic_setup.reason:
DefaultSiteRenderer is a Plexus/Sisu component whose rendering paths depend on injected Doxia, VelocityComponent, ParserModuleManager, and PlexusContainer fields. The artifact's resolved dependencies include Sisu but omit Guice as a provided dependency; upstream 2.0.0 tests use plexus-testing to cre
...[truncated]

Doxia parser modules are no longer all in doxia-core; the 2.0.0 POM explicitly notes parser modules must be declared so their component descriptors are picked up. Adding the APT module enables representative site-document rendering rather than only inert context/resource checks.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding parser modules broadens the test classpath; keep assertions focused on doxia-site-renderer behavior rather than testing the parser module itself.
Local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative because Sisu/Plexus component discovery can be sensitive to classpath differences.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful rendering coverage for doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 needs Plexus/Sisu injection and a Doxia parser module beyond the plain artifact scaffold.
